stem: A GPS navigation navigation system uses 2 parallel satellite links for redundancy. The position fixed requires at least one satellite link to function. satellite link 1 has a reliability of 97%. satellite link 2 has a reliability between 65% and 80% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the position fixed?

ground_truth.answer: P(position fixed) ranges from 0.9895 to 0.994. Point estimate: 0.9919.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 satellite links fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.03 × 0.35 = 0.0105. P(works) = 0.9895.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.03 × 0.2 = 0.006. P(works) = 0.994.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
